title: [kimi-review] 167f01e: [SEVERITY: low] This commit removes the only in-function sec
description:
Commit: 167f01e6d
Message: fix(oscore): clean deprecated oscore_ctx_lookup per security audit

Finding: [SEVERITY: low] This commit removes the only in-function security warning for a function that still performs the hazardous behavior (stack-copying key material via `memcpy(ctx_out, ctx, ...)`). The justification — that the warning now lives in the header — cannot be verified from this diff. If the header documentation is missing or incomplete, callers lose the only notice that (a) the copy can't be used with protect/unprotect and (b) `oscore_ctx_wipe()` is mandatory. Recommend verifying the header actually contains this warning, and better yet, adding a compiler-enforced deprecation (`__attribute__((deprecated))` / `__DEPRECATED_MACRO`) or removing `oscore_ctx_lookup()` entirely rather than deleting its warning comment.
